context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements reorder point, safety stock, order quantity, and urgency classification logic according to the demand forecasting skill. The scenario provides raw daily sales and inventory data and asks for replenishment recommendations.

checklist.name: Z-score 1.645 used
checklist.description: Uses Z = 1.645 (95% service level) as the default z-score for safety stock calculation

checklist.name: Safety stock formula
checklist.description: Safety stock is computed as Z * residualStdDev * sqrt(leadTimeDays), not a simpler heuristic

checklist.name: Reorder point formula
checklist.description: Reorder point = avgDailyDemand * leadTimeDays + safetyStock (ceiling applied)

checklist.name: Default lead time 7 days
checklist.description: When a product has no specified lead time, defaults to 7 days

checklist.name: Urgency warning threshold
checklist.description: Urgency is 'warning' when daysOfSupply < leadTimeDays * 2 (and not critical)

checklist.name: daysOfSupply formula
checklist.description: daysOfSupply = floor(quantity_on_hand / avgDailyDemand), returns 999 (or equivalent large number) when avgDailyDemand is 0

checklist.name: Pending PO subtraction
checklist.description: quantity_on_order (open purchase orders) is subtracted from the recommended order quantity before output

checklist.name: High-velocity Z adjustment
checklist.description: Uses Z = 2.05 (98% service level) for products flagged as high-velocity/high-margin

checklist.name: Slow-mover Z adjustment
checklist.description: Uses Z = 1.28 (90% service level) for products flagged as slow movers

checklist.name: Report filters ok urgency
checklist.description: The output report / recommendations list does NOT include products with 'ok' urgency — only 'critical' and 'warning' items are present
